registers.name: CR1
registers.description: PWR control register 1

fields.name: LPMS
fields.description: Low-power mode selection These bits select the low-power mode entered when the CPU enters the Deepsleep mode. 10x: Standby mode (Standby mode also entered if LPMS = 11X in PWR_CR1 with BREN = 1 in PWR_BDCR1) 11x: Shutdown mode if BREN = 0 in PWR_BDCR1

enum_values.name: Standby
enum_values.description: Standby mode (Standby mode also entered if LPMS = 11X in PWR_CR1 with BREN = 1 in PWR_BDCR1)

enum_values.name: Shutdown
enum_values.description: Shutdown mode if BREN = 0 in PWR_BDCR1

fields.name: RRSB1
fields.description: SRAM2 page 1 retention in Stop 3 and Standby modes This bit is used to keep the SRAM2 page 1 content in Stop 3 and Standby modes. The SRAM2 page 1 corresponds to the first 8 Kbytes of the SRAM2 (from SRAM2 base address to SRAM2 base address + 0x1FFF). Note: This bit has no effect in Shutdown mode.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: SRAM2 page1 content not retained in Stop3 and Standby modes

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: SRAM2 page1 content retained in Stop 3 and Standby modes

fields.name: RRSB2
fields.description: SRAM2 page 2 retention in Stop 3 and Standby modes This bit is used to keep the SRAM2 page 2 content in Stop 3 and Standby modes. The SRAM2 page 2 corresponds to the last 56 Kbytes of the SRAM2 (from SRAM2 base address + 0x2000 to SRAM2 base address + 0xFFFF). Note: This bit has no effect in Shutdown mode.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: SRAM2 page2 content not retained in Stop3 and Standby modes

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: SRAM2 page2 content retained in Stop 3 and Standby modes

fields.name: ULPMEN
fields.description: BOR ultra-low power mode This bit is used to reduce the consumption by configuring the BOR in discontinuous mode. This bit must be set to reach the lowest power consumption in the low-power modes.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: BOR level 0 operating in continuous (normal) mode in Standby mode

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: BOR level 0 operating in discontinuous (ultra-low power) mode in Standby mode

fields.name: SRAM1PD
fields.description: SRAM1 power down This bit is used to reduce the consumption by powering off the SRAM1.

fields.name: SRAM2PD
fields.description: SRAM2 power down This bit is used to reduce the consumption by powering off the SRAM2.

fields.name: SRAM4PD
fields.description: SRAM4 power down This bit is used to reduce the consumption by powering off the SRAM4.

registers.name: CR2
registers.description: PWR control register 2

fields.name: SRAM1PDS1
fields.description: SRAM1 page 1 (64 Kbytes) power-down in Stop modes (Stop 0, 1, 2, 3)

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: SRAM1 page x content retained in Stop modes

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: SRAM1 page x content lost in Stop modes

fields.name: SRAM1PDS2
fields.description: SRAM1 page 2 (64 Kbytes) power-down in Stop modes (Stop 0, 1, 2, 3)

fields.name: SRAM1PDS3
fields.description: SRAM1 page 3 (64 Kbytes) power-down in Stop modes (Stop 0, 1, 2, 3)

fields.name: SRAM2PDS1
fields.description: SRAM2 page 1 (8 Kbytes) power-down in Stop modes (Stop 0, 1, 2) Note: The SRAM2 page 1 retention in Stop 3 is controlled by RRSB1 bit in PWR_CR1.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: SRAM2 page x content retained in Stop modes

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: SRAM2 page x content lost in Stop modes

fields.name: SRAM2PDS2
fields.description: SRAM2 page 2 (56 Kbytes) power-down in Stop modes (Stop 0, 1, 2) Note: The SRAM2 page 2 retention in Stop 3 is controlled by RRSB2 bit in PWR_CR1.

fields.name: SRAM4PDS
fields.description: SRAM4 power-down in Stop modes (Stop 0, 1, 2, 3)

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: SRAM4 content retained in Stop modes

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: SRAM4 content lost in Stop modes

fields.name: ICRAMPDS
fields.description: ICACHE SRAM power-down in Stop modes (Stop 0, 1, 2, 3)

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: ICACHE SRAM content retained in Stop modes

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: ICACHE SRAM content lost in Stop modes

fields.name: DC1RAMPDS
fields.description: DCACHE1 SRAM power-down in Stop modes (Stop 0, 1, 2, 3)

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: DCACHE1 SRAM content retained in Stop modes

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: DCACHE1 SRAM content lost in Stop modes

fields.name: PRAMPDS
fields.description: FMAC, FDCAN and USB peripherals SRAM power-down in Stop modes (Stop 0, 1, 2, 3)

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: FMAC, FDCAN, and USB/OTG_FS/OTG_HS SRAM content retained in Stop modes

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: FMAC, FDCAN, and USB/OTG_FS/OTG_HS SRAM content lost in Stop modes

fields.name: SRAM4FWU
fields.description: SRAM4 fast wakeup from Stop 0, Stop 1 and Stop 2 modes This bit is used to obtain the best trade-off between low-power consumption and wakeup time. SRAM4 wakeup time increases the wakeup time when exiting Stop 0, 1 and 2 modes, and also increases the LPDMA access time to SRAM4 during Stop modes.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: SRAM4 enters low-power mode in Stop 0/1/2 modes (source biasing for lower-power consumption)

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: SRAM4 remains in normal mode in Stop 0/1/2 modes (higher consumption but no SRAM4 wake-up time)

fields.name: FLASHFWU
fields.description: Flash memory fast wakeup from Stop 0 and Stop 1 modes This bit is used to obtain the best trade-off between low-power consumption and wakeup time when exiting the Stop 0 or Stop 1 modes. When this bit is set, the Flash memory remains in normal mode in Stop 0 and Stop 1 modes, which offers a faster startup time with higher consumption.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Flash memory enters low-power mode in Stop 0/1 modes (lower-power consumption)

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Flash memory remains in normal mode in Stop 0/1 modes (faster wake-up time)

fields.name: SRDRUN
fields.description: SmartRun domain in Run mode

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: SmartRun domain AHB3 and APB3 clocks disabled by default in Stop 0/1/2 modes

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: SmartRun domain AHB3 and APB3 clocks kept enabled in Stop 0/1/2 modes

registers.name: CR3
registers.description: PWR control register 3

fields.name: REGSEL
fields.description: Regulator selection Note: REGSEL is reserved and must be kept at reset value in packages without SMPS.

fields.name: FSTEN
fields.description: Fast soft start

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: LDO/SMPS fast startup disabled (limited inrush current)

registers.name: VOSR
registers.description: PWR voltage scaling register

fields.name: BOOSTRDY
fields.description: EPOD booster ready This bit is set to 1 by hardware when the power booster startup time is reached. The system clock frequency can be switched higher than 50 MHz only after this bit is set.

fields.name: VOSRDY
fields.description: Ready bit for VCORE voltage scaling output selection

enum_values.name: NotReady
enum_values.description: Not ready, voltage level < VOS selected level

enum_values.name: Ready
enum_values.description: Ready, voltage level ≥ VOS selected level

fields.name: VOS
fields.description: Voltage scaling range selection This field is protected against non-secure access when SYSCLKSEC = 1 in RCC_SECCFGR. It is protected against unprivileged access when SYSCLKSEC = 1 in RCC_SECCFGR and SPRIV = 1 in PWR_PRIVCFGR, or when SYSCLKSEC = 0 and NSPRIV = 1.

fields.name: BOOSTEN
fields.description: EPOD booster enable This bit is protected against non-secure access when SYSCLKSEC = 1 in RCC_SECCFGR. It is protected against unprivileged access when SYSCLKSEC = 1 in RCC_SECCFGR and SPRIV = 1 in PWR_PRIVCFGR, or when SYSCLKSEC = 0 and NSPRIV = 1. This bit must be set in range 1 and range 2 before increasing the system clock frequency above 50 MHz. This bit is reset when going into Stop modes (0, 1, 2, 3).

registers.name: SVMCR
registers.description: PWR supply voltage monitoring control register

fields.name: PVDE
fields.description: Power voltage detector enable

fields.name: PVDLS
fields.description: Power voltage detector level selection These bits select the voltage threshold detected by the power voltage detector:

enum_values.name: PVDIN
enum_values.description: External input analog voltage PVD_IN (compared internally to VREFINT)

fields.name: UVMEN
fields.description: VDDUSB independent USB voltage monitor enable

fields.name: IO2VMEN
fields.description: VDDIO2 independent I/Os voltage monitor enable

fields.name: AVM1EN
fields.description: VDDA independent analog supply voltage monitor 1 enable (1.6 V threshold)

fields.name: AVM2EN
fields.description: VDDA independent analog supply voltage monitor 2 enable (1.8 V threshold)

fields.name: USV
fields.description: VDDUSB independent USB supply valid This bit is used to validate the VDDUSB supply for electrical and logical isolation purpose. Setting this bit is mandatory to use the USB peripheral. If VDDUSB is not always present in the application, the VDDUSB voltage monitor can be used to determine whether this supply is ready or not.

enum_values.name: NotPresent
enum_values.description: VDDUSB not present: logical and electrical isolation is applied to ignore this supply

fields.name: IO2SV
fields.description: VDDIO2 independent I/Os supply valid This bit is used to validate the VDDIO2 supply for electrical and logical isolation purpose. Setting this bit is mandatory to use PG[15:2]. If VDDIO2 is not always present in the application, the VDDIO2 voltage monitor can be used to determine whether this supply is ready or not.

enum_values.name: NotPresent
enum_values.description: VDDIO2 not present: logical and electrical isolation is applied to ignore this supply

fields.name: ASV
fields.description: VDDA independent analog supply valid This bit is used to validate the VDDA supply for electrical and logical isolation purpose. Setting this bit is mandatory to use the analog peripherals. If VDDA is not always present in the application, the VDDA voltage monitor can be used to determine whether this supply is ready or not.

enum_values.name: NotPresent
enum_values.description: VDDA not present: logical and electrical isolation is applied to ignore this supply

registers.name: WUCR1
registers.description: PWR wakeup control register 1

fields.name: WUPEN1
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP1 enable

fields.name: WUPEN2
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP2 enable

fields.name: WUPEN3
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP3 enable

fields.name: WUPEN4
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP4 enable

fields.name: WUPEN5
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP5 enable

fields.name: WUPEN6
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP6 enable

fields.name: WUPEN7
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP7 enable

fields.name: WUPEN8
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP8 enable

registers.name: WUCR2
registers.description: PWR wakeup control register 2

fields.name: WUPP1
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP1 polarity. This bit must be configured when WUPEN1 = 0.

fields.name: WUPP2
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP2 polarity This bit must be configured when WUPEN2 = 0.

fields.name: WUPP3
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP3 polarity This bit must be configured when WUPEN3 = 0.

fields.name: WUPP4
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP4 polarity This bit must be configured when WUPEN4 = 0.

fields.name: WUPP5
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP5 polarity This bit must be configured when WUPEN5 = 0.

fields.name: WUPP6
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP6 polarity This bit must be configured when WUPEN6 = 0.

fields.name: WUPP7
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP7 polarity This bit must be configured when WUPEN7 = 0.

fields.name: WUPP8
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP8 polarity This bit must be configured when WUPEN8 = 0.

registers.name: WUCR3
registers.description: PWR wakeup control register 3

fields.name: WUSEL1
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP1 selection This field must be configured when WUPEN1 = 0.

fields.name: WUSEL2
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP2 selection This field must be configured when WUPEN2 = 0.

fields.name: WUSEL3
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP3 selection This field must be configured when WUPEN3 = 0.

fields.name: WUSEL4
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP4 selection This field must be configured when WUPEN4 = 0.

fields.name: WUSEL5
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP5 selection This field must be configured when WUPEN5 = 0.

fields.name: WUSEL6
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP6 selection This field must be configured when WUPEN6 = 0.

fields.name: WUSEL7
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP7 selection This field must be configured when WUPEN7 = 0.

fields.name: WUSEL8
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP8 selection This field must be configured when WUPEN8 = 0.

registers.name: BDCR1
registers.description: PWR Backup domain control register 1

fields.name: BREN
fields.description: Backup RAM retention in Standby and VBAT modes When this bit is set, the backup RAM content is kept in Standby and VBAT modes. If BREN is reset, the backup RAM can still be used in Run, Sleep and Stop modes. However, its content is lost in Standby, Shutdown and VBAT modes. This bit can be written only when the regulator is LDO, which must be configured before switching to SMPS. Note: Backup RAM cannot be preserved in Shutdown mode.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Backup RAM content lost in Standby and VBAT modes

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Backup RAM content preserved in Standby and VBAT modes

fields.name: MONEN
fields.description: Backup domain voltage and temperature monitoring enable

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Backup domain voltage and temperature monitoring disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Backup domain voltage and temperature monitoring enabled

registers.name: BDCR2
registers.description: PWR Backup domain control register 2

fields.name: VBE
fields.description: VBAT charging enable

fields.name: VBRS
fields.description: VBAT charging resistor selection

enum_values.name: R_5k
enum_values.description: Charge VBAT through a 5 kOhm resistor

enum_values.name: R_1k5
enum_values.description: Charge VBAT through a 1.5 kOhm resistor

registers.name: DBPR
registers.description: PWR disable Backup domain register

fields.name: DBP
fields.description: Disable Backup domain write protection In reset state, all registers and SRAM in Backup domain are protected against parasitic write access. This bit must be set to enable the write access to these registers.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Write access to backup domain disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Write access to backup domain enabled

registers.name: UCPDR
registers.description: PWR USB Type-C™ and Power Delivery register

registers.name: SECCFGR
registers.description: PWR security configuration register

fields.name: WUP1SEC
fields.description: WUP1 secure protection

enum_values.name: NonSecure
enum_values.description: Bits related to WKUPx pin in PWR_WUCR1, PWR_WUCR2, PWR_WUCR3, and PWR_WUSCR can be read and written with secure or nonsecure access

enum_values.name: Secure
enum_values.description: Bits related to WKUPx pin in PWR_WUCR1, PWR_WUCR2, PWR_WUCR3, and PWR_WUSCR can be read and written only with secure access

fields.name: WUP2SEC
fields.description: WUP2 secure protection

fields.name: WUP3SEC
fields.description: WUP3 secure protection

fields.name: WUP4SEC
fields.description: WUP4 secure protection

fields.name: WUP5SEC
fields.description: WUP5 secure protection

fields.name: WUP6SEC
fields.description: WUP6 secure protection

fields.name: WUP7SEC
fields.description: WUP7 secure protection

fields.name: WUP8SEC
fields.description: WUP8 secure protection

fields.name: LPMSEC
fields.description: Low-power modes secure protection

enum_values.name: NonSecure
enum_values.description: PWR_CR1, PWR_CR2 and CSSF in the PWR_SR can be read and written with secure or nonsecure access

enum_values.name: Secure
enum_values.description: PWR_CR1, PWR_CR2, and CSSF in the PWR_SR can be read and written only with secure access

fields.name: VDMSEC
fields.description: Voltage detection and monitoring secure protection

enum_values.name: NonSecure
enum_values.description: PWR_SVMCR and PWR_CR3 can be read and written with secure or nonsecure access

enum_values.name: Secure
enum_values.description: PWR_SVMCR and PWR_CR3 can be read and written only with secure access

fields.name: VBSEC
fields.description: Backup domain secure protection

enum_values.name: NonSecure
enum_values.description: PWR_BDCR1, PWR_BDCR2, and PWR_DBPR can be read and written with secure or nonsecure access

enum_values.name: Secure
enum_values.description: PWR_BDCR1, PWR_BDCR2, and PWR_DBPR can be read and written only with secure access

fields.name: APCSEC
fields.description: Pull-up/pull-down secure protection

enum_values.name: NonSecure
enum_values.description: PWR_APCR can be read and written with secure or nonsecure access

enum_values.name: Secure
enum_values.description: PWR_APCR can be read and written only with secure access

registers.name: PRIVCFGR
registers.description: PWR privilege control register

fields.name: SPRIV
fields.description: PWR secure functions privilege configuration This bit is set and reset by software. It can be written only by a secure privileged access.

enum_values.name: NonSecure
enum_values.description: Read and write to PWR secure functions can be done by privileged or unprivileged access

enum_values.name: Secure
enum_values.description: Read and write to PWR secure functions can be done by privileged access only

fields.name: NSPRIV
fields.description: PWR non-secure functions privilege configuration This bit is set and reset by software. It can be written only by privileged access, secure or non-secure.

enum_values.name: NonSecure
enum_values.description: Read and write to PWR nonsecure functions can be done by privileged or unprivileged access

enum_values.name: Secure
enum_values.description: Read and write to PWR nonsecure functions can be done by privileged access only

registers.name: SR
registers.description: PWR status register

fields.name: CSSF
fields.description: Clear Stop and Standby flags This bit is protected against non-secure access when LPMSEC = 1 in PWR_SECCFGR. This bit is protected against unprivileged access when LPMSEC = 1 and SPRIV = 1 in PWR_PRIVCFGR, or when LPMSEC = 0 and NSPRIV = 1. Writing 1 to this bit clears the STOPF and SBF flags.

enum_values.name: Clear
enum_values.description: Clear the STOPF and SBF flags

fields.name: STOPF
fields.description: Stop flag This bit is set by hardware when the device enters a Stop mode, and is cleared by software by writing 1 to the CSSF bit.

enum_values.name: NoStop
enum_values.description: The device did not enter any Stop mode

enum_values.name: Stop
enum_values.description: The device entered a Stop mode

fields.name: SBF
fields.description: Standby flag This bit is set by hardware when the device enters the Standby mode, and is cleared by writing 1 to the CSSF bit, or by a power-on reset. It is not cleared by the system reset.

enum_values.name: NoStandby
enum_values.description: The device did not enter Standby mode

registers.name: SVMSR
registers.description: PWR supply voltage monitoring status register

fields.name: REGS
fields.description: Regulator selection

fields.name: PVDO
fields.description: VDD voltage detector output

enum_values.name: EqualOrAboveThreshold
enum_values.description: VDD is equal or above the PVD threshold selected by PVDLS[2:0]

enum_values.name: BelowThreshold
enum_values.description: VDD is below the PVD threshold selected by PVDLS[2:0]

fields.name: ACTVOSRDY
fields.description: Voltage level ready for currently used VOS

enum_values.name: NotReady
enum_values.description: VCORE is above or below the current voltage scaling provided by ACTVOS[1:0]

enum_values.name: Ready
enum_values.description: VCORE is equal to the current voltage scaling provided by ACTVOS[1:0]

fields.name: ACTVOS
fields.description: VOS currently applied to VCORE This field provides the last VOS value.

fields.name: VDDUSBRDY
fields.description: VDDUSB ready

enum_values.name: BelowThreshold
enum_values.description: VDDUSB is below the threshold of the VDDUSB voltage monitor

enum_values.name: EqualOrAboveThreshold
enum_values.description: VDDUSB is equal or above the threshold of the VDDUSB voltage monitor

fields.name: VDDIO2RDY
fields.description: VDDIO2 ready

enum_values.name: BelowThreshold
enum_values.description: VDDIO2 is below the threshold of the VDDIO2 voltage monitor

enum_values.name: EqualOrAboveThreshold
enum_values.description: VDDIO2 is equal or above the threshold of the VDDIO2 voltage monitor

fields.name: VDDA1RDY
fields.description: VDDA ready versus 1.6V voltage monitor

enum_values.name: BelowThreshold
enum_values.description: VDDA is below the threshold of the VDDA voltage monitor 1 (around 1.6 V)

enum_values.name: EqualOrAboveThreshold
enum_values.description: VDDA is equal or above the threshold of the VDDA voltage monitor 1 (around 1.6 V)

fields.name: VDDA2RDY
fields.description: VDDA ready versus 1.8 V voltage monitor

enum_values.name: BelowThreshold
enum_values.description: VDDA is below the threshold of the VDDA voltage monitor 2 (around 1.8 V)

enum_values.name: AboveThreshold
enum_values.description: VDDA is equal or above the threshold of the VDDA voltage monitor 2 (around 1.8 V)

registers.name: BDSR
registers.description: PWR Backup domain status register

fields.name: VBATH
fields.description: Backup domain voltage level monitoring versus high threshold

fields.name: TEMPL
fields.description: Temperature level monitoring versus low threshold

fields.name: TEMPH
fields.description: Temperature level monitoring versus high threshold

registers.name: WUSR
registers.description: PWR wakeup status register

fields.name: WUF1
fields.description: Wakeup flag 1 This bit is set when a wakeup event is detected on WKUP1 pin. This bit is cleared by writing 1 in the CWUF1 bit of PWR_WUSCR when WUSEL ≠ 11, or by hardware when WUPEN1 = 0.

enum_values.name: NoWakeup
enum_values.description: No wakeup event occurred on WKUPx pin

enum_values.name: Wakeup
enum_values.description: A wakeup event occurred on WKUPx pin

fields.name: WUF2
fields.description: Wakeup flag 2 This bit is set when a wakeup event is detected on WKUP2 pin. This bit is cleared by writing 1 in the CWUF2 bit of PWR_WUSCR when WUSEL ≠ 11, or by hardware when WUPEN2 = 0.

fields.name: WUF3
fields.description: Wakeup flag 3 This bit is set when a wakeup event is detected on WKUP3 pin. This bit is cleared by writing 1 in the CWUF3 bit of PWR_WUSCR when WUSEL ≠ 11, or by hardware when WUPEN3 = 0.

fields.name: WUF4
fields.description: Wakeup flag 4 This bit is set when a wakeup event is detected on WKUP4 pin. This bit is cleared by writing 1 in the CWUF4 bit of PWR_WUSCR when WUSEL ≠ 11, or by hardware when WUPEN4 = 0.

fields.name: WUF5
fields.description: Wakeup flag 5 This bit is set when a wakeup event is detected on WKUP5 pin. This bit is cleared by writing 1 in the CWUF5 bit of PWR_WUSCR when WUSEL ≠ 11, or by hardware when WUPEN5 = 0.

fields.name: WUF6
fields.description: Wakeup flag 6 This bit is set when a wakeup event is detected on WKUP6 pin. This bit is cleared by writing 1 in the CWUF6 bit of PWR_WUSCR when WUSEL ≠ 11, or by hardware when WUPEN6 = 0. If WUSEL = 11, this bit is cleared by hardware when all internal wakeup source are cleared.

fields.name: WUF7
fields.description: Wakeup flag 7 This bit is set when a wakeup event is detected on WKUP7 pin. This bit is cleared by writing 1 in the CWUF7 bit of PWR_WUSCR when WUSEL ≠ 11, or by hardware when WUPEN7 = 0. If WUSEL = 11, this bit is cleared by hardware when all internal wakeup source are cleared.

fields.name: WUF8
fields.description: Wakeup flag 8 This bit is set when a wakeup event is detected on WKUP8 pin. This bit is cleared by writing 1 in the CWUF8 bit of PWR_WUSCR when WUSEL ≠ 11, or by hardware when WUPEN8 = 0. If WUSEL = 11, this bit is cleared by hardware when all internal wakeup source are cleared.

registers.name: WUSCR
registers.description: PWR wakeup status clear register

fields.name: CWUF1
fields.description: Wakeup flag 1 Writing 1 to this bit clears the WUF1 flag in PWR_WUSR.

enum_values.name: Clear
enum_values.description: Clear the WUFx flag in PWR_WUSR

fields.name: CWUF2
fields.description: Wakeup flag 2 Writing 1 to this bit clears the WUF2 flag in PWR_WUSR.

fields.name: CWUF3
fields.description: Wakeup flag 3 Writing 1 to this bit clears the WUF3 flag in PWR_WUSR.

fields.name: CWUF4
fields.description: Wakeup flag 4 Writing 1 to this bit clears the WUF4 flag in PWR_WUSR.

fields.name: CWUF5
fields.description: Wakeup flag 5 Writing 1 to this bit clears the WUF5 flag in PWR_WUSR.

fields.name: CWUF6
fields.description: Wakeup flag 6 Writing 1 to this bit clears the WUF6 flag in PWR_WUSR.

fields.name: CWUF7
fields.description: Wakeup flag 7 Writing 1 to this bit clears the WUF7 flag in PWR_WUSR.

fields.name: CWUF8
fields.description: Wakeup flag 8 Writing 1 to this bit clears the WUF8 flag in PWR_WUSR.

registers.name: APCR
registers.description: PWR apply pull configuration register

fields.name: APC
fields.description: Apply pull-up and pull-down configuration When this bit is set, the I/O pull-up and pull-down configurations defined in PWR_PUCRx and PWR_PDCRx are applied. When this bit is cleared, PWR_PUCRx and PWR_PDCRx are not applied to the I/Os.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: PWR_PUCRx and PWR_PDCRx are not applied to the I/Os

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: I/O pull-up and pull-down configurations defined in PWR_PUCRx and PWR_PDCRx are applied

registers.name: PUCRA
registers.description: PWR port A pull-up control register

fields.name: PU15
fields.description: Port A pull-up bit 15 When set, this bit activates the pull-up on PA15 when the APC bit is set in PWR_APCR. The pull-up is not activated if the corresponding PD15 bit is also set.

registers.name: PDCRA
registers.description: PWR port A pull-down control register

fields.name: PD14
fields.description: Port A pull-down bit 14 When set, this bit activates the pull-down on PA14 when the APC bit is set in PWR_APCR.
